answers.where: Old Mine scout/result direction target: dominant cave entrance scene with integrated RPG chrome.
answers.action: Use this as the next visual/UX target only if the lead accepts the scene-first route/scout grammar.
answers.response: The target replaces NEXT SLICE scaffolding with visible route, threat, resource, reward, and log surfaces.
answers.reward: The player can understand why scouting matters: cave threat, ember shard resource, depth marker, XP/gold/ring reward preview.
answers.game_look: This reads closer to a fantasy browser RPG target than the rejected runtime screenshot, but it is a fake shot and not runtime-ready.

issues.text: Direction fake shot is not sliced runtime UI and cannot be used as final packed UI art.

next: If accepted, derive runtime layout/assets from this target while preserving Y-up layout semantics; if rejected, update the digest before coding.
